query: I just got handed a real-world KVR config file from one of our biggest customers. Can you add it as a golden test fixture under the kvr package and wire up a round-trip test against it? File is at ~/Downloads/customer-prod.kvr.
we keep regressing on this one weird TLV blob — every time someone touches the encoder it breaks for this specific input. drop ./tlv-bug-2024-11.bin into pkg/tlv/testdata as a regression-guard please
found a parser bug on this gnarly INI file (./Downloads/sample.ini). pin it as a test case for ./pkg/ini so we don't regress once i fix the parser
Add the file at /tmp/edge_case.toml to the toml package's testdata/ and wire it to the existing round-trip test table.
harden my graphql parser against this real schema we got from a user — file is at ./schemas/from-user-issue-127.graphql, package is at pkg/graphql
i want this binary file pinned as a golden fixture for my elf decoder. it round-trips today and i want to make sure it always does. ./samples/static-binary.elf, package is at /home/me/code/elf-go
ok so i've got like 5 real protobuf bytes blobs from prod traffic captures and i want each one as a regression fixture for the proto package — start with ./capture-1.pb
drop this in as a test

so the customer sent us their config (at ~/inbox/their-config.kvr) and it broke our parser yesterday. i fixed the parser. now i need to make sure it never breaks on that file again — handle this for me
scaffold a new go file library package called yaml so i can start working on a YAML parser/printer
audit my pkg/kvr package against its SPEC.md — i want to know what's missing before i ship
extract a SPEC.md from the existing pkg/json package — i need to capture what the format actually does before i refactor
implement the integer literal grammar from SPEC.md section 3.4 in pkg/kvr/parser.go — the spec just got updated and the package is missing it
i need to set up testdata for our gorm models — there's a fixture loader somewhere in this repo right? add some seed user records under tests/fixtures/users.json
we need golden snapshot tests for the React component library. when a snapshot diverges the test should fail — set up jest-snapshot for ./packages/ui
copy ./Downloads/spec-draft.kvr to docs/examples/ — the docs site renders kvr files in that folder as syntax highlighted examples
explain the round-trip property for parser-printer libraries to me — why is parse → print → parse → AST equality the right shape and not byte equality?
open the file at testdata/sample.kvr and read the first 20 lines into context — i want to understand what's in it before i write a test
go ahead and add error handling to the Encode function in pkg/tlv/encoder.go — it's panicking on truncated input right now
we're designing a new binary file format for shipping telemetry — give me your recommended layout for header, records, and trailer with checksums
